title: Declared vs Implementation Drift
description: Scan for public claims that implementation does not satisfy: CLI help, docs, schemas, snapshots, AGENTS/skills text, package scripts, exports, i18n copy, generated templates. Focus on concrete mismatches with file:line evidence. Deduplicate mentally against existing registry; prefer genuinely new or sharper findings.
findings: 确认 4 条新的 declared-vs-implementation drift：experimental HTTP 包继续引用 server 已移除 exports；doctor 公开文案硬编码 48 checks 但实现只有约 46/47；tooling manifest 的 protected-token 输入路径指向不存在的 root templates；server npm metadata 仍描述旧 .fabric/agents.meta 知识库。
issues_found: [{"title":"Experimental HTTP APIs import server barrel exports that the server explicitly removed","severity":"medium","description":"packages/server/src/index.ts says getKnowledge/readAgentsMeta are no longer re-exported after agents.meta decolo, but the quarantined HTTP API sources still import readAgentsMeta/getKnowledge from @fenglimg/fabric-server. If this package is restored or typechecked with current server d.ts, those endpoints target a public API that no longer exists.","location":"packages/server-http-experimental/src/api/knowledge.ts:1","fix_direction":"Either remove/retire the legacy /api/rules and /api/rules/context endpoints, or update them to current store-aware APIs exported by @fenglimg/fabric-server; add a direct typecheck path for the quarantined package before restoration.","affected_components":["packages/server-http-experimental/src/api/knowledge.ts","packages/server-http-experimental/src/api/ledger.ts","packages/server-http-experimental/src/api/knowledge-context.ts","packages/server/src/index.ts"],"tags":["declared-vs-implementation","server-http-quarantine","export-drift"]},{"title":"Doctor CLI/README still advertise 48 checks after the implementation changed","severity":"medium","description":"README and doctor CLI help/TLDR hard-code '48 checks', but runDoctorReport currently builds 46 unconditional checks plus one optional promote-ledger check, so a clean report can claim all 48 checks are green even though the implementation no longer has 48 checks in that array.","location":"packages/cli/src/commands/doctor.ts:773","fix_direction":"Stop hard-coding the check count in CLI/docs; derive it from report.checks.length or rewrite copy to avoid an exact number. Update README occurrences and any snapshot expectations together.","affected_components":["packages/cli/src/commands/doctor.ts","README.md","packages/server/src/services/doctor.ts"],"tags":["declared-vs-implementation","cli-copy","doctor"]},{"title":"Tooling manifest protected-token inputs point at obsolete template roots","severity":"medium","description":"docs/tooling-manifest.json declares lint-protected-tokens inputs as templates/bootstrap/** and templates/claude-skills/**, but the actual script reads packages/cli/templates/bootstrap and packages/cli/templates/skills, and there is no root templates directory. A machine-readable source_of_truth manifest will mislead automation about what the quality gate validates.","location":"docs/tooling-manifest.json:72","fix_direction":"Update the manifest paths/names to packages/cli/templates/bootstrap and packages/cli/templates/skills, or explicitly mark bootstrap as absent and covered by packages/shared/src/templates/bootstrap-canonical.ts tests.","affected_components":["docs/tooling-manifest.json","scripts/lint-protected-tokens.ts","packages/cli/templates"],"tags":["declared-vs-implementation","tooling-manifest","quality-gate"]},{"title":"Published server package metadata still describes retired .fabric/agents.meta storage","severity":"low","description":"@fenglimg/fabric-server package.json describes the server as managing a .fabric knowledge base plus agents.meta.json, while current project policy says knowledge entries live only in mounted stores and server index comments say the co-location agents.meta surface is no longer re-exported. This stale npm metadata presents the old storage model to package consumers.","location":"packages/server/package.json:4","fix_direction":"Refresh the package description to mention mounted stores, stdio MCP, event ledger/metrics, and store-aware knowledge retrieval; avoid advertising .fabric/knowledge or agents.meta.json as managed storage.","affected_components":["packages/server/package.json","npm metadata","store-only documentation"],"tags":["declared-vs-implementation","package-metadata","store-only-drift"]}]

title: Producer Consumer Drift
description: Scan for exported capabilities, config fields, generated artifacts, event types, snapshots, or schema properties that are produced but unused, consumed incompatibly, or duplicated across packages. Require file:line evidence on both producer and consumer sides when possible.
findings: 发现 1 个新的 producer/consumer drift：store-aware MCP 契约/测试声称 6 个工具输出 store edge，但实际 runtime schemas/services 只输出旧字段或 alias-only store 信息。
issues_found: [{"title":"Store-aware MCP contract advertises provenance and written_to_store fields that runtime outputs do not produce","severity":"high","description":"`MCP_STORE_AWARE_CONTRACTS` and its tests lock a 6-tool store-aware contract where READ tools surface `global_ref`/`provenance` and WRITE tools echo `written_to_store`, and the parity matrix describes recall/sections as returning `store_uuid + alias + local_id + global_ref`. Actual output schemas/services are incompatible: `knowledgeSectionsOutputSchema.rules[]` only has `stable_id/level/path/body`, `recallOutputSchema.rules[]` only adds optional `store:{alias}`, and `FabExtractKnowledgeOutputSchema`/`extractKnowledge` return only `pending_path` and `idempotency_key`. Clients relying on the exported contract can believe store-qualified citation/write-target data exists while MCP responses omit it, especially under multi-store shadowing.","location":"Producer: packages/shared/src/schemas/mcp-store-contracts.ts:17, packages/shared/test/store/mcp-store-contracts.test.ts:11, packages/shared/src/parity/parity-matrix.json:167; Consumer/runtime: packages/shared/src/schemas/api-contracts.ts:329, packages/shared/src/schemas/api-contracts.ts:480, packages/shared/src/schemas/api-contracts.ts:826, packages/server/src/services/recall.ts:196, packages/server/src/services/extract-knowledge.ts:464","fix_direction":"Either make the real MCP output schemas and services emit the promised store edge (`global_ref`, `provenance`, and `written_to_store`) for all contracted tools, or narrow `MCP_STORE_AWARE_CONTRACTS`/parity descriptions/tests to the fields actually shipped; add contract tests that validate runtime tool outputs against the exported store-aware contract rather than only validating standalone helper schemas.","affected_components":["@fenglimg/fabric-shared MCP schemas","@fenglimg/fabric-server MCP services","parity matrix","store-aware contracts"],"tags":["producer-consumer-drift","mcp-contract","store-aware","schema-runtime-drift"]}]

title: Round Trip Invariant Failures
description: Scan install/uninstall, bind/switch-write, route-write, archive/review, sync/restore, parse/render, doctor fix/check, recall/get-sections flows for broken round-trip or non-idempotent behavior. Require a concrete invariant and file:line evidence.
findings: 发现 2 个 round-trip invariant 问题：project-scoped route-write 写入后不会被 archive/review 写路径实际使用；fab_recall include_related 的 bare related-id 契约与 store-qualified 候选 id 不匹配。已避开现有 switch-write snapshot、doctor 集成、G11 stale 等重复 issue。
issues_found: [{"title":"Project route-write is ignored by archive/review write-target resolution","severity":"high","description":"Invariant: after `fabric store route-write project:fabric-v2 platform`, a team-layer archive in a repo bound to `active_project=fabric-v2` should land in the routed `platform` store and record matching visibility. The shared resolver supports project routes (`packages/shared/test/store/privacy-boundary.test.ts:55`), and the CLI persists routes (`packages/cli/src/store/store-ops.ts:476`), but server write-target helpers always resolve the literal `team` scope for team-layer writes (`packages/server/src/services/cross-store-write.ts:60`, `packages/server/src/services/cross-store-write.ts:115`) before later stamping `semantic_scope: project:<active_project>` (`packages/server/src/services/cross-store-write.ts:123`). `extractKnowledge` and review promotion/layer-flip use those helpers (`packages/server/src/services/extract-knowledge.ts:359`, `packages/server/src/services/review.ts:607`, `packages/server/src/services/review.ts:964`), so the config write round-trip silently falls back to the default/active team store instead of the route.","location":"packages/server/src/services/cross-store-write.ts:60","fix_direction":"Derive an effective write scope once (`personal` for personal, otherwise `project:${active_project}` when present, else `team`) and pass that scope into `resolveWriteTarget` for both target dir and visibility metadata. Add an integration test that seeds two writable stores, sets `write_routes: [{scope:'project:fabric-v2', store:'platform'}]` plus `active_project:'fabric-v2'`, runs `extractKnowledge` and review approve, then asserts pending/canonical paths, counters, and `visibility_store` land in `platform`.","affected_components":["cross-store write","fab_extract_knowledge","fab_review approve/modify-layer","store route-write","StoreResolver"],"tags":["round-trip-invariants","route-write","archive-review","store-routing"]},{"title":"fab_recall include_related only works with store-qualified related IDs despite bare-id contract","severity":"medium","description":"Invariant: a frontmatter edge authored as `related: [KT-GLD-0001]` should round-trip through plan candidates and `fab_recall({include_related:true})` to fetch that neighbor. The schema/builder document and parse bare stable ids (`packages/shared/src/schemas/agents-meta.ts:80`, `packages/server/src/services/knowledge-meta-builder.ts:1070`, `packages/server/src/services/knowledge-meta-builder.ts:1245`), while cross-store recall emits store-qualified candidate ids via `entry.qualifiedId` (`packages/server/src/services/cross-store-recall.ts:163`). `recall` compares `candidate.description.related` directly against store-qualified `candidateIds` (`packages/server/src/services/recall.ts:132`, `packages/server/src/services/recall.ts:137`, `packages/server/src/services/recall.ts:148`), and the test only passes because it seeds a non-contract `related: [team:KT-GLD-0001]` (`packages/server/src/services/recall.test.ts:196`). Real bare related edges therefore neither expand nor produce the next-step nudge.","location":"packages/server/src/services/recall.ts:132","fix_direction":"Normalize related ids before comparison: accept bare local ids by qualifying them with the source candidate's store alias when possible, or compare against both local and qualified id maps. Update recall tests to seed `related: [KT-GLD-0001]` and add cross-store ambiguity coverage so include_related behavior is deterministic.","affected_components":["fab_recall","include_related","knowledge graph related edges","cross-store recall","agents-meta frontmatter parsing"],"tags":["round-trip-invariants","recall","related-edges","store-qualified-id"]}]

title: State Error Isolation Risks
description: Scan for state leaks, process.env mutation, global caches, partial writes, race conditions, stale locks, silent failures, misleading fallback/error behavior, and unbounded state. Require file:line evidence and explain the failure mode.
findings: 发现 2 个未登记的 state/error isolation 风险：shutdown drain 单槽等待器会在跨信号并发时覆盖等待状态；metrics flush window 使用全局 interval，跨 projectRoot 串状态。
issues_found: [{"title":"In-flight drain uses a single waiter slot, so concurrent shutdown drains can overwrite each other","severity":"medium","description":"createShutdownHandler is per-signal, so SIGTERM and SIGINT handlers can both enter the first-invocation path and call tracker.drain concurrently. The tracker stores only one resolveDrained callback; the later drain overwrites the earlier one, so when active requests exit only the newest waiter resolves immediately while the earlier shutdown path waits until its deadline and may then run fsync/flush/close/exit out of phase with the other handler.","location":"packages/server/src/services/in-flight-tracker.ts:10","fix_direction":"Represent drain waiters as a Set/array and resolve all pending waiters when active.size reaches 0, or centralize shutdown dedup across all signals so only one drain can be active; add a regression test for concurrent drain() calls before the final exit().","affected_components":["packages/server/src/services/in-flight-tracker.ts","packages/server/src/index.ts","server shutdown"],"tags":["state-error-isolation","shutdown-race","global-state"]},{"title":"Metrics flush window is global mutable state shared across project roots","severity":"low","description":"startMetricsFlush stores each root's timer in flushTimers, but also writes the selected interval to the module-level flushIntervalMs. flushMetrics later labels rows with options.windowMs ?? flushIntervalMs, so starting a second projectRoot with a different interval changes the window label used by manual/final flushes for every other project even though counters are per-project.","location":"packages/server/src/services/metrics.ts:43","fix_direction":"Store interval/window metadata per projectRoot alongside each timer, or pass the captured interval into every timer and stop-handle flush; add a test that starts two roots with different intervals and verifies each emitted row keeps its own window.","affected_components":["packages/server/src/services/metrics.ts","metrics.jsonl","multi-project server state"],"tags":["state-error-isolation","state-leak","metrics"]}]

title: Test Gate Drift
description: Scan tests, snapshots, fixtures, coverage thresholds, package scripts, release gates, and mocks for stale or weaker contracts than production behavior. Prefer findings where tests can pass while behavior is wrong or drifted.
findings: 发现 2 个 test gate drift：CI perf gate 使用 legacy/minimal fixture 可漏掉 mounted-store 冷启动退化；root 暴露的 rc6:gate 已与当前文件/配置漂移，作为 release helper 会产生失真 gate 信号。
issues_found: [{"title":"CI perf benchmark uses a legacy minimal fixture instead of the mounted-store workflow","severity":"medium","description":"The CI perf gate claims to measure interactive `fabric doctor` and SessionStart cold-start latency, but setupCliFixture only creates `.fabric/agents.meta.json` and `.fabric/events.jsonl` in a synthetic temp root. It never creates a current mounted store, store.json, project binding, or resolved-bindings snapshot, so store resolver / read-set / bindings regressions can pass this p95 gate while production-shaped projects slow down.","location":"scripts/perf-benchmark.mjs:54","fix_direction":"Add a second production-shaped benchmark fixture that creates a Fabric home, mounted store with store.json and knowledge entries, project fabric-config binding, and bindings snapshot; gate or at least report p95 for that mounted-store doctor/hook path from CI.","affected_components":["scripts/perf-benchmark.mjs",".github/workflows/ci.yml","fabric doctor cold start","SessionStart hook perf gate","mounted store workflow"],"tags":["test-gate-drift","perf-gate","store-fixture-drift"]},{"title":"Exposed rc6 gate is stale against current hook/config files","severity":"medium","description":"The root package still exposes `pnpm rc6:gate`, but the gate re-runs a stale rc5 anchor and checks current files against outdated assumptions. With build/tests skipped, it fails on rc5 legacy checks, missing Cursor SessionStart/PreToolUse slots, and a deleted `packages/cli/src/commands/hooks.ts` path before it can provide a useful release signal. This makes the named gate misleading and likely to be bypassed rather than trusted.","location":"scripts/rc6-coverage-gate.mjs:439","fix_direction":"Either retire rc5:gate/rc6:gate from package.json, or update rc6 checks to current hook config ownership and command files, then add a lightweight test/CI assertion that exposed gate scripts execute successfully with FABRIC_GATE_SKIP_BUILD=1 and FABRIC_GATE_SKIP_TESTS=1.","affected_components":["package.json rc6:gate","scripts/rc6-coverage-gate.mjs","scripts/rc5-coverage-gate.mjs","hook config release checks"],"tags":["test-gate-drift","release-gate","stale-script"]}]

title: Dedup + Issue Creation
description: Aggregate all perspective findings. Deduplicate against existing .workflow/issues/issues.jsonl by normalized title, location, affected component, and root cause. Use source=discover for new records. Append only unique records with ISS-YYYYMMDD-NNN IDs.
findings: pre-dedup 11 -> unique new 11; internal dupes 0; existing dupes 0. No candidate duplicates found against registry.
